title: lonchocarpus sumiderensis
intro: Lonchocarpus sumiderensis, also known as the Sumidero tree, is a plant species native to Mexico, specifically found in the state of Chiapas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of lonchocarpine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus sumiderensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared in herbal teas or infusions, and its bark is used to create a poultice, which is applied topically to affected areas.
